title: leontodon autumnalis subsp. pratensis
intro: Leontodon autumnalis subsp. pratensis, also known as autumn hawk's beard or hawkbit, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow dandelion-like flowers and leafy stem. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Leontodon autumnalis subsp. pratensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a raw vegetable or used in salads, although its usage should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
